wikidata_candidates.label: Wilhelm Wessner
wikidata_candidates.reasoning: The two records describe individuals with different given names (Charles vs. Wilhelm) and completely different professions (Controls Engineering vs. Botany). There is no overlapping information to suggest they are the same person.

results.possibleIdentity: Charles Thomas Wessner Jr. (1930–1993) was a controls engineer and business owner who was active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. During this period, he was employed by M.& T. Chemicals (also known as Metal & Thermit Chemicals) at their facility in Farmingdale, New Jersey. He is documented as a technical member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization that facilitated collaborations between the artistic and technical communities. A resident of Freehold Township, Monmouth County, New Jersey, for approximately 30 years, he later founded and operated the C.T. Equipment Company in Lakewood, New Jersey, starting around 1975. He was also a veteran of the Korean War, having served in the U.S. Army from 1953 to 1954.
